name: San Juan
summary: San Juan is generally safe for tourists, particularly in main areas like Old San Juan and Condado, with strong police presence and US-level healthcare infrastructure. While crime rates are higher than mainland US cities, most incidents are petty theft or drug-related violence concentrated in specific neighborhoods away from tourist zones.
quickVerdict: San Juan offers a relatively safe Caribbean experience with the conveniences of US territory status. Tourist areas are well-policed and most visitors encounter no serious problems. However, exercise standard urban precautions as property crime occurs, and be aware that certain neighborhoods should be avoided. The city's LGBTQ-friendly atmosphere and familiar infrastructure make it accessible for diverse travelers.

neighborhoods.name: Old San Juan
neighborhoods.description: Historic walled city with strong police presence, well-lit streets, and constant foot traffic. Very walkable with numerous attractions, restaurants, and shops. Generally safe day and night due to heavy tourism and security.

neighborhoods.name: Condado
neighborhoods.description: Upscale beachfront district with luxury hotels, restaurants, and shopping. Good lighting and regular patrols. Popular with tourists and locals. Exercise normal precautions but generally secure area.

neighborhoods.name: Ocean Park
neighborhoods.description: Trendy residential area near beaches with boutique hotels and cafes. Quieter than Condado but well-maintained. Safe for walking during day, use caution at night in less populated areas.

neighborhoods.name: Isla Verde
neighborhoods.description: Beach area near airport with resorts and restaurants. Generally safe tourist zone but can feel isolated at night. Stay near main hotel areas and avoid empty beach sections after dark.

neighborhoods.name: Santurce
neighborhoods.description: Arts district with galleries, markets, and nightlife. Mixed area with both trendy spots and rougher sections. Safe during day in main areas, exercise increased caution at night and know your exact destination.

neighborhoods.name: La Perla
neighborhoods.description: Historic but impoverished neighborhood adjacent to Old San Juan. High crime rates and drug activity. Strongly advised to avoid despite its colorful appearance and fame from music videos. Visit only with organized tours if at all.

scams.name: Beach Theft
scams.description: Thieves target unattended belongings while tourists swim or sunbathe. Items stolen from bags, chairs, or beach towels when tourists are distracted.
scams.howToAvoid: Never leave valuables unattended. Use waterproof pouches for essentials. Designate someone to watch belongings or use hotel safes instead of bringing valuables to beach.

scams.name: Taxi Overcharging
scams.description: Drivers may not use meters, take longer routes, or claim no change for large bills. Some may quote inflated fixed prices to tourists.
scams.howToAvoid: Use Uber or Lyft when possible. For regular taxis, insist meter is used or agree on fare beforehand. Have small bills ready and research typical rates.

scams.name: Rental Car Break-ins
scams.description: Thieves target rental cars with visible items inside, especially in parking lots near tourist attractions. GPS units and bags are common targets.
scams.howToAvoid: Keep nothing visible in car. Use paid, supervised parking when possible. Don't leave GPS units or charging cables visible. Park in well-lit, busy areas.

scams.name: Overpriced Tours
scams.description: Street promoters offer bio-bay tours or island excursions at inflated prices, sometimes with poor quality service or hidden fees.
scams.howToAvoid: Book tours through reputable companies or hotels. Compare prices online beforehand. Be wary of high-pressure sales tactics from street promoters.

soloFemale.overview: San Juan is one of the safer Caribbean destinations for solo female travelers, with many women reporting positive experiences. The city's US territory status provides familiar infrastructure and legal protections. Tourist areas like Old San Juan and Condado have good lighting and police presence. However, standard precautions are still necessary, especially regarding nightlife and transportation.
tips: Return to accommodation by sunset or stick to well-lit, busy areas after dark
Use Uber or reputable taxis rather than walking alone at night
Limit alcohol consumption to maintain awareness of surroundings
Share location with someone and avoid posting real-time locations on social media
Stay in main tourist areas and avoid neighborhoods like La Perla

nightSafety.overview: Nightlife in San Juan is generally safe in tourist zones with good police presence and lighting. Old San Juan and Condado have vibrant night scenes with restaurants, bars, and clubs operating safely. However, crime rates increase after dark, particularly muggings and theft. Some areas become unsafe at night and should be avoided entirely.
tips: Stick to busy, well-lit areas like Old San Juan's main streets and Condado hotel zones
Travel in groups when possible, especially when bar-hopping
Use Uber, Lyft, or hotel-arranged transportation rather than walking long distances
Avoid La Perla, isolated beach areas, and poorly lit residential streets
Keep valuables minimal and stay alert to your surroundings

transport.metro: San Juan has the Tren Urbano metro system connecting some areas, but coverage is limited. Generally safe and clean but doesn't serve major tourist areas directly.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Lyft operate throughout San Juan and are widely recommended. Reliable, safe, and convenient for tourists. Available 24/7 in main areas with reasonable wait times.
transport.taxis: Licensed taxis are white with roof lights and generally safe. Some drivers may not use meters or may overcharge tourists. Agree on fare beforehand or insist on meter usage.
transport.tips: Use rideshare apps when possible for transparency and convenience. Public buses (AMA) exist but can be unreliable. Rental cars are good for exploring outside San Juan but parking can be challenging in Old San Juan. Walking is excellent in Old San Juan and Condado during daylight hours.

customs: Dress modestly when visiting churches and avoid beachwear in town areas
Tip 15-20% at restaurants and bars, as this is expected and often a main source of income for service workers
Learning basic Spanish phrases is appreciated, though English is widely understood in tourist areas
Be respectful of local environment, especially protected areas like El Yunque rainforest
Puerto Ricans value politeness and formal greetings when meeting people for the first time

health.overview: San Juan has US-level healthcare with modern hospitals and clinics. Most US health insurance plans work in Puerto Rico. The healthcare system is among the best in the Caribbean region.
health.water: Tap water is safe to drink throughout San Juan as it meets US standards. Bottled water is widely available if preferred.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required beyond standard US recommendations. Routine vaccines should be up to date. Consider hepatitis A/B for some travelers.
health.altitude: San Juan is at sea level with tropical climate year-round. Hurricane season runs June-November with August-October being peak risk period. Dengue and Zika are present but risk is low with proper mosquito precautions.

emergency.usEmbassy: No US Embassy needed as Puerto Rico is US territory. For federal assistance: 1-888-407-4747

faq.q: Is San Juan safe for tourists?
faq.a: Yes, San Juan is generally safe for tourists, especially in main areas like Old San Juan, Condado, and Isla Verde. These areas have strong police presence and are well-lit. However, exercise normal urban precautions and avoid certain neighborhoods like La Perla.

faq.q: Is San Juan safe at night?
faq.a: Tourist areas like Old San Juan and Condado are relatively safe at night with good lighting and police presence. However, crime rates do increase after dark, so stick to busy areas, travel in groups when possible, and use Uber or taxis for transportation.

faq.q: Is San Juan safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: San Juan is one of the safer Caribbean destinations for solo female travelers. Many women visit safely, especially in tourist zones. Use standard precautions like limiting alcohol, avoiding isolated areas at night, and using rideshare for transportation.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in San Juan?
faq.a: Avoid La Perla neighborhood despite its colorful appearance, as it has high crime rates. Also avoid poorly lit residential areas at night, isolated beach sections after dark, and areas away from main tourist zones like parts of Santurce late at night.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in San Juan?
faq.a: The Tren Urbano metro is safe but has limited coverage. Uber and Lyft are widely recommended and very reliable. Regular taxis are generally safe but may overcharge tourists. Public buses exist but can be unreliable for tourists.
